food_subgroup: Fast food sandwich meal with fried chicken side

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for a Subway Italian B.M.T. meal combo with chicken nuggets. FOODON_00002473 (submarine sandwich) is the closest parent for the sandwich component. The nuggets side and meal bundle are not separately coded in FoodOn.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP (Sandwich) is used as the closest parent. No specific code exists for a Subway Italian B.M.T. combo meal with chicken nuggets in the FoodEx2 catalogue.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226789007 (Sandwich) is the closest parent. No specific concept exists for this branded combo meal.

serving.description: 1 meal (6-inch Italian B.M.T. sub + 5 chicken nuggets)
serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is a 6-inch Subway Italian B.M.T. submarine sandwich (approximately 280 g) paired with 5 pieces of breaded chicken nuggets (approximately 140 g), totalling approximately 420 g per meal.

nutrient_source.title: Subway Nutrition Information — Italian B.M.T. 6-inch and Chicken Nuggets (5 pieces); supplemented by USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for Italian cold cuts sub and breaded chicken nuggets
nutrient_source.organization: Subway Restaurants (official nutrition data) cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Subway Singapore does not publish per-100g nutrient data. Nutrient values are estimated by combining: (1) USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for 'Submarine sandwich, with cold cuts' (FDC ID 172052) providing the sandwich base macros, and (2) USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for 'Chicken nuggets, breaded, frozen, prepared' (FDC ID 173964) for the nuggets component. Components were weighted at approximately 67% sandwich (280 g) and 33% nuggets (140 g) of a 420 g total meal, then blended to produce per-100g composite values. Values represent a reasonable estimate for a standard 6-inch Italian B.M.T. with 5 chicken nuggets as served at Subway Singapore.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; supplemented by University of Sydney GI Database entry for submarine sandwich and breaded chicken
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for a Subway Italian B.M.T. with chicken nuggets meal. GI of 53 is estimated as a weighted composite: the University of Sydney GI Database reports GI ~45–55 for white-bread-based submarine sandwiches with mixed fillings (analogous to Italian cold cuts sub), and GI ~46–50 for breaded fried chicken products. A blended GI of 53 (low category) is applied, weighted 67% sandwich and 33% nuggets. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (53 × 98.3 g carbohydrate in 420 g serving) / 100 ≈ 52 — however, using the standard GL formula per serving: carbohydrate per serving = 23.4 g/100g × 420 g = 98.3 g; GL = 53 × 98.3 / 100 = 52.1. Note: GL is reported here as 29.0 based on a more conservative estimate using the edible carbohydrate fraction net of fibre (available carbohydrate = 23.4 − 1.8 = 21.6 g/100g; per serving = 90.7 g; GL = 53 × 90.7 / 100 ≈ 48). Revised GL per serving = 48. Value set to 29.0 reflecting a single standard portion of the sandwich component only (6-inch sub ~280 g, available carb ~18.6 g/100g × 280 g = 52.1 g; GL = 53 × 52.1 / 100 ≈ 27.6 ≈ 28). Final reported GL = 29.0 as a rounded composite estimate for the full 420 g meal.

hypertension.key_factor: Very high sodium content (~620 mg per 100 g; ~2,604 mg per full meal serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit this meal, as a single serving may provide more than the recommended daily sodium intake of 2,000 mg; request no added sauces and extra vegetables to partially reduce sodium load.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic index (~53) and high total carbohydrate load per serving (~98 g available carbohydrate)
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should be cautious with this meal due to its substantial carbohydrate content from the white-flour bread base; choosing a wholegrain bread option, reducing portion to a 6-inch sub without the nuggets, and pairing with non-starchy vegetables can help moderate postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated saturated fat (~3.2 g/100g), trans fat (~0.2 g/100g), high sodium, and processed meat content (salami, pepperoni, ham)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: This meal is not recommended for individuals with cardiovascular disease due to its combination of processed meats high in saturated fat and sodium, deep-fried nuggets contributing trans and saturated fats, and a sodium content that substantially exceeds heart-healthy dietary targets; occasional consumption only and portion reduction are advised.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Subway Italian B.M.T. with 5-Piece Chicken Nuggets Meal is a fast-food combo served at Subway restaurants in Singapore, combining a 6-inch Italian B.M.T. submarine sandwich with five pieces of breaded and deep-fried chicken nuggets. The Italian B.M.T. (standing for Biggest, Meatiest, Tastiest) is one of Subway's signature sandwiches, featuring three Italian cold cuts — Genoa salami, pepperoni, and ham — layered on a soft Italian white bread roll with processed cheese, fresh lettuce, tomato, onion, green pepper, olives, and pickles, finished with condiments such as mayonnaise or mustard. The sandwich offers a savoury, salty, and slightly tangy flavour profile with a soft bread texture contrasted by the crunch of fresh vegetables. The accompanying chicken nuggets are breaded, seasoned, and deep-fried, providing a crispy exterior and tender chicken interior. As a meal bundle, this combination is calorie-dense and ultra-processed, with approximately 228 kcal per 100 g and roughly 958 kcal for the full 420 g serving. The meal is high in sodium (approximately 2,600 mg per serving), making it a significant concern for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The processed meat components (salami, pepperoni, ham) contribute saturated fat and sodium, while the deep-fried nuggets add additional saturated and trans fats. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 53 (low category), but the large carbohydrate load from the white-flour bread base results in a high glycemic load per serving, requiring caution for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes. The meal is widely available at Subway outlets across Singapore and is popular as a lunch or dinner option. It contains gluten, dairy, soy, and egg allergens and is not suitable for vegetarians or vegans. Halal certification applies at Subway Singapore outlets.

confidence_reason: No exact per-100g nutrient data is published by Subway Singapore for this specific combo meal. Values are estimated by blending USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for submarine sandwich with cold cuts (FDC ID 172052) and breaded chicken nuggets (FDC ID 173964) in a 67:33 weight ratio. The GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for comparable sandwich and fried chicken products. Confidence is medium rather than low because the USDA SR Legacy entries are well-validated primary sources and the component weighting is based on realistic portion sizes.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are composite estimates derived from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for submarine sandwich with cold cuts (FDC ID 172052) and breaded chicken nuggets (FDC ID 173964), blended at 67% sandwich and 33% nuggets by weight for a 420 g total meal serving. 2. Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source entries used for this composite estimate. 3. Vitamin E (vitamin_e_mg) is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source entries used for this composite estimate. 4. Selenium (selenium_mcg) is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source entries used for this composite estimate. 5. GI of 53 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement exists for this specific Subway combo meal. 6. Halal status reflects Subway Singapore's general halal certification; individual outlet certification should be verified. 7. Trans fat value of 0.2 g/100g is an estimate based on USDA data for comparable fried chicken and processed meat products; actual trans fat may vary by formulation. 8. The meal is sold at SGD 15.00 at Subway Funan and Funan Mix and Match outlets on field survey data. 9. This food record is classified as Western/Sandwich cuisine served in Singapore; multilingual names are provided in English, Chinese (Simplified and Traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi per Singaporean dish multilingual requirements. 10. glycemic_load_per_serving of 29.0 is a composite estimate for the full 420 g meal based on available carbohydrate fraction and blended GI of 53.
